---
title: "Thin Paspalum (Paspalum setaceum)"
slug: "paspalum-setaceum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475159622/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Thin Paspalum"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475159622/medium.jpg" alt="Thin Paspalum" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lmcconachie">
lmcconachie</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264609614">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=122592)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry to mesic habitats such as flatwoods.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its slender stems and narrow leaves, with delicate seed heads composed of a few thin, spike-like branches. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic habitats such as flatwoods, sandhills, and open disturbed areas. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to groundcover in native grassland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](http://inaturalist.org/taxa/122592-Paspalum-setaceum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3304)
